L1.area: Two-dimensional array data structure element insertion and memory management logic

L2.description: A use-after-free (double free) vulnerability exists in operations that insert data from an iterator into a two-dimensional array. When elements are shifted via unsafe pointer operations to make space for new entries, ownership of existing elements is duplicated. If the iterator panics or its reported length does not match the actual number of elements produced, elements in the array can be freed twice, or uninitialized and previously freed memory can be exposed.

L3.area: Row and column insertion methods in the two-dimensional array implementation
L3.description: A use-after-free (double free) vulnerability exists in the row and column insertion operations that accept an ExactSizeIterator. When elements in the underlying storage are shifted via unsafe pointer operations to make space for new entries, ownership of existing elements is duplicated. If the iterator panics during consumption or its reported length does not match the actual number of elements produced, elements in the array can be freed twice, or uninitialized and previously freed memory may be exposed.

ground_truth.reason: The insert_row and insert_col functions shift existing elements via unsafe pointer copy to make space for new entries, duplicating ownership. If the iterator panics or reports an incorrect length, elements can be double-freed or uninitialized memory exposed.
